args.code:
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime

cases_data = locals()['var_function-call-2021392460420602180']
owner_assignment_data = locals()['var_function-call-9267804943343700567']

df_cases = pd.DataFrame(cases_data)
df_owner_assignment = pd.DataFrame(owner_assignment_data)

# Clean Ids: remove leading '#' and strip whitespace
df_cases['id'] = df_cases['id'].astype(str).str.lstrip('#').str.strip()
df_cases['ownerid'] = df_cases['ownerid'].astype(str).str.lstrip('#').str.strip()
df_owner_assignment['caseid__c'] = df_owner_assignment['caseid__c'].astype(str).str.lstrip('#').str.strip()

# Convert date columns to datetime objects
df_cases['createddate'] = pd.to_datetime(df_cases['createddate'])
df_cases['closeddate'] = pd.to_datetime(df_cases['closeddate'])

# Filter out cases that are not closed or have no owner (ownerid is null or empty)
df_closed_cases = df_cases[(df_cases['closeddate'].notna()) & (df_cases['ownerid'].notna()) & (df_cases['ownerid'] != '')]

# Identify transferred cases: cases with more than one 'Owner Assignment'
transferred_cases = df_owner_assignment['caseid__c'].value_counts()
transferred_cases = transferred_cases[transferred_cases > 1].index.tolist()

# Filter out transferred cases from closed cases
df_filtered_cases = df_closed_cases[~df_closed_cases['id'].isin(transferred_cases)]

# Calculate handle time in seconds
df_filtered_cases['handle_time'] = (df_filtered_cases['closeddate'] - df_filtered_cases['createddate']).dt.total_seconds()

# Group by ownerid and calculate average handle time and case count
agent_stats = df_filtered_cases.groupby('ownerid').agg(
    avg_handle_time=('handle_time', 'mean'),
    case_count=('id', 'count')
).reset_index()

# Filter for agents who processed more than one case
filtered_agents = agent_stats[agent_stats['case_count'] > 1]

if not filtered_agents.empty:
    # Find the agent with the lowest average handle time
    lowest_avg_handle_time_agent = filtered_agents.sort_values(by='avg_handle_time').iloc[0]
    result_agent_id = lowest_avg_handle_time_agent['ownerid']
else:
    result_agent_id = "No agent found with more than one case and valid handle time."


print('__RESULT__:')
print(pd.json_normalize({'agent_id': result_agent_id}).to_json(orient='records'))

args.code:
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime

cases_data = locals()['var_function-call-2021392460420602180']
owner_assignment_data = locals()['var_function-call-9267804943343700567']

df_cases = pd.DataFrame(cases_data)
df_owner_assignment = pd.DataFrame(owner_assignment_data)

# Clean Ids: remove leading '#' and strip whitespace
df_cases['id'] = df_cases['id'].astype(str).str.lstrip('#').str.strip()
df_cases['ownerid'] = df_cases['ownerid'].astype(str).str.lstrip('#').str.strip()
df_owner_assignment['caseid__c'] = df_owner_assignment['caseid__c'].astype(str).str.lstrip('#').str.strip()

# Replace 'None' strings with actual None values for date columns
df_cases['createddate'] = df_cases['createddate'].replace('None', None)
df_cases['closeddate'] = df_cases['closeddate'].replace('None', None)

# Convert date columns to datetime objects, coercing errors to NaT
df_cases['createddate'] = pd.to_datetime(df_cases['createddate'], errors='coerce')
df_cases['closeddate'] = pd.to_datetime(df_cases['closeddate'], errors='coerce')

# Filter out cases that are not closed or have no owner (ownerid is null or empty)
df_closed_cases = df_cases[(df_cases['closeddate'].notna()) & (df_cases['ownerid'].notna()) & (df_cases['ownerid'] != '')]

# Identify transferred cases: cases with more than one 'Owner Assignment'
transferred_cases = df_owner_assignment['caseid__c'].value_counts()
transferred_cases = transferred_cases[transferred_cases > 1].index.tolist()

# Filter out transferred cases from closed cases
df_filtered_cases = df_closed_cases[~df_closed_cases['id'].isin(transferred_cases)]

# Calculate handle time in seconds
df_filtered_cases['handle_time'] = (df_filtered_cases['closeddate'] - df_filtered_cases['createddate']).dt.total_seconds()

# Group by ownerid and calculate average handle time and case count
agent_stats = df_filtered_cases.groupby('ownerid').agg(
    avg_handle_time=('handle_time', 'mean'),
    case_count=('id', 'count')
).reset_index()

# Filter for agents who processed more than one case
filtered_agents = agent_stats[agent_stats['case_count'] > 1]

if not filtered_agents.empty:
    # Find the agent with the lowest average handle time
    lowest_avg_handle_time_agent = filtered_agents.sort_values(by='avg_handle_time').iloc[0]
    result_agent_id = lowest_avg_handle_time_agent['ownerid']
else:
    result_agent_id = "No agent found with more than one case and valid handle time."


print('__RESULT__:')
print(pd.json_normalize({'agent_id': result_agent_id}).to_json(orient='records'))
